item.title: Don't get the hate on it
item.content:encoded: Seems pretty straightforward, not really understanding all the dislike towards it. I personally enjoy it. It also makes you think about the Foundation's intentions and why they are taking down groups that are trying to support mental health. Reminds me of SCP-006. Upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I wanted to like this. I really wanted to like this. But I just can't. This article has all the pieces to be something great, but I never felt that this went far enough. I think this idea, while decent, is never fully explored to the degree that I would like. The GoI, which I think has some narrative potential, but again, is never taken to the degree that I would personally enjoy. The activation phrases never held the emotional weight that I think they should have had, along with the interview.

Downvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The footnote was completely useless. Thanks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: But then you'd lose the B52s reference.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Downvoted, after initially no-voting.

I sorta like the imagery here, and it's pretty well polished, but there's really not a lot of effort put into making this particularly interesting. Just into polishing the technical writing.

It's pretty mediocre, and one of many articles that will probably never get improved because at +44 (including a bump from being one-day featured for Valentine's Day for little apparent reason beyond being thematically appropriate) it's not going to be in danger of deletion for at least a year or two if ever.

item.title: Suggestion
item.content:encoded:
What I think would of made more sense, instead of saying 'If someone remains inside SCP-1937, they would be infected by SCP-1937-2', you could put 'If someone remains inside SCP-1937, they are then classified as SCP-1937-2', or something like that.
(Don't word it like that though.)
Overall, I can see all the things I want to point out have been posted on already, so I won't go over the things you've heard enough of.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Agreeing with the others, the addendum was kind of a mess. Downvote til' it's cleaned up.
This made me laugh though:

Fredricks: How do you mean?

Yannatos: [silence for approximately thirty seconds]

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can't figure out how I feel about this one, so Imma just leave it alone.

item.title: Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
This article is up for review. Please read it, this needs more attention. :D

Well, I think the interview is well written, but it feels really scattered and lacking focus in the main article and addenda. I wasn't sure what the author was trying to do, and ended up just really confused by the end. There are several really neat ideas that are fighting each other for dominance. One needs to kill the others and shine in the article. I think this is one that started out 100% different than it is now, then went through at least three different versions, and wasn't edited enough to focus on one of those three ideas, if that makes sense. It feels two major revisions short of a great article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I can see where you're coming from and have edited some of the stylistic complaints you've raised. As for the greater detail, I'm heavily considering doing a rewrite of this in the future that will extend the ideas presented. To answer some apparent curiosities:

  • It was originally supposed to be reminiscent of a certain myth, but that's more of an Easter egg than anything else now.
  • The GoI was never meant to really extend past this article, but I can see your points and will probably add to their section if I decide on rewriting this a bit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No vote. This feels like it is on the cusp of being a good idea… but reading it I kinda got the impression that I was looking at a box of puzzle pieces and trying to guess what the picture would look like when they were all fitted together. It doesn't really take any of the ideas (the enigmatic GOI, the effect, the causes of/longterm implications of the effect) far enough. I have some stylistic nitpicks, too—

Personnel are not to spend more than thirty (30) minutes within SCP-1937 for purposes other than D-Class testing.

This doesn't really make sense. So they CAN spend MORE than thirty minutes "within SCP-1937" if they ARE conducting D-class testing? (Why would they be within it if they are using D-class?) Because, grammatically, that's what this sentence means.

SCP-1937 is a building located in ███████, Greece.

I don't like over-elaborate descriptions, but 1) there's no image and 2) you don't give us an idea how big this thing is or what it looks like. This article could use some atmosphere.

Upon speaking these phrases, a voice matching that of the infected subject will immediately respond. This voice will always manifest in manner matching the infected subject's intonation and inflection while speaking the trigger phrase.

This is repetitive. Combine the sentences.

Furthermore, it was discovered that several now-contained items were being used as additional services to treat drug addiction, eating disorders, and "abnormal sexualities," respectively.

You introduce something that could be interesting, here, but the idea just seems disconnected from the object being described… I feel like you're trying to make some sinister implications about the potential uses or origins of these items and they're just not coming across.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: But does it make you a Horrible PersonTM?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wanting a voice that constantly reaffirms you makes you a Horrible Person.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Good job. An outlook on a GoI that's not a major one, gets liquidated and crossed off the list.
That said.
Is it bad I almost wouldn't mind being infected?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it except the 2nd paragraph in description is a little hard to understand until you keep reading the addendums. Explain it a little more clearly so we don't have to keep reading to understand.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the concept a lot, but I'm not really crazy about the GoI. It's less the fact that there's people involved, and more the fact that they made a group, rather than just some guys dealing good feels like drugs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I hate to burst the bubble I created, but

After interrogation and capture of the remainder of the Hope Crusade's personnel and anomalies, all members were administered Class-C amnestics, released, and monitored for a subsequent six (6) months in order to ensure the prevention of the reformation of this group.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Always a fan of new GoI's being introduced.d

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can think of a couple reasons. One of them would not be telling people it's permanent, because all their other SCPs fade, so one could presume this one was intended to, as well. The mercenary mindset. The more charitable interpretation is that while it takes 30 minutes for the effects to permanently manifest, more time spent in the shack equals a larger 'dictionary' of phrases.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the GoI as well. Something that isn't outright evil, or screwups like Manna.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I approve of this GoI, and the skip itself is pretty interesting and unusual.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Self-Love Shack

i see what you did there

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I would've preferred there be no GoI, just a shed that developed a rumor that turned out to be true. Brings a kind of wonder into the picture that a GoI wouldn't.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it, but why are people paying to be exposed to the shed per hour if its effect is permanent?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This should be renamed "Masturbation Station."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Strangely enough, I think this might have worked better if an existing GoI was used creatively instead of this new one we know nothing about nor care about. If it was, for example, an enterprising local brand manager of MC&D, a corrupt Manna Charitable Foundation memeber, or even some CI operation (much like real terrorist organization do things like pouching).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think the interview helped, but it still doesn't do much for me.
